methodology.bank_to_bank_sla: Leg1 (Funds Received → Transaction Completed median, 397s) + Leg2 (payout network P80 per destination)
methodology.leg1_source: Amplitude funnel: Funds Received (senderAddressCountryCode=GB) → Transaction Completed (senderCountry=GB, paymentMethod=bank_account). Last 30 days. n=3058. Median 397s, average 32,357s.
methodology.leg2_source: Weekly UK payout report (Jimmy), P80 per (currency, destination) across bank payout methods (Sepa/Swift/FPS/RIA/Domestic), volume-weighted.
methodology.rationale: Transaction Created → Transaction Completed would include the customer's bank-clearing time which we don't control. Using Funds Received as Leg 1 start excludes bank clearing, so the measured Leg 1 is our actual post-funding processing time. Adding this to Leg 2 P80 gives a realistic end-to-end SLA.

code_changes.change: Add optional DestinationCountry property (kept old ctor for backward compat)
Composite key (ccy, country) + TryFindEntry with fallback to currency default
Read optional DestinationCountry column; group by (ccy, country); preserve Ria priority per group
Overloads accept destinationCountry; fallback to currency default if no match
New overload threads destinationCountry through; old signature delegates with null (back-compat)
156 existing rows preserved + 35 per-destination improvement rows + trailing DestinationCountry column
3 new tests: destination-specific match, unknown-country fallback, no-country backward compat
Added Xe artifactory feeds (xemt, gdt-nuget) alongside nuget.org so restore works locally
New CLI to preview Quote-response lead-time string for any (country, ccy, dest, pm, dm, time)

rowSelection.description: When UK.csv has multiple rows for the same currency, the launchpad loader picks one — Ria variant wins.
rowSelection.is_ria_explanation: c[13] is the CCY column. If the cell contains "Ria" (case-insensitive) — i.e. "Ria CCY" — the row is flagged IsRia=true.
